samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Implement an 8-bit multiplier using pipelined processing. Utilize a clock signal (clk), an active-low reset signal (rst_n), an input enable signal (mul_en_in), and provide an output enable signal (mul_en_out) and the product output (mul_out).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Utilizes a pipeline architecture to enhance performance. Consists of multiple stages, including input control, input registers, partial product generation, partial sum calculation, and final product calculation.</implementation>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_en_out_reg">Register to hold the output enable signal.</reg>
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_en_out_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_en_out_reg <= {mul_en_out_reg[6:0], mul_en_in};
            end
        </always>
        <assign mul_en_out = mul_en_out_reg[7];>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_a_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplicand.</reg>
        <reg id="mul_b_reg" width_description="[7:0]">Register to hold the multiplier.</reg>
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_a_reg <= 0;
                mul_b_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (mul_en_out_reg[7]) begin
                    mul_a_reg <= mul_a;
                    mul_b_reg <= mul_b;
                end
            end
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <wire id="temp[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the first partial product.</wire>
        <assign temp[0] = {{8{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg[0]} & {{8{1'b0}}, mul_b_reg[0]};>
        <wire id="temp[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the second partial product.</wire>
        <assign temp[1] = {{7{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg[1:0]} & {{7{1'b0}}, mul_b_reg[1:0]};>
        <wire id="temp[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the third partial product.</wire>
        <assign temp[2] = {{6{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg[2:0]} & {{6{1'b0}}, mul_b_reg[2:0]};>
        <wire id="temp[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the fourth partial product.</wire>
        <assign temp[3] = {{5{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg[3:0]} & {{5{1'b0}}, mul_b_reg[3:0]};>
        <wire id="temp[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the fifth partial product.</wire>
        <assign temp[4] = {{4{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg[4:0]} & {{4{1'b0}}, mul_b_reg[4:0]};>
        <wire id="temp[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the sixth partial product.</wire>
        <assign temp[5] = {{3{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg[5:0]} & {{3{1'b0}}, mul_b_reg[5:0]};>
        <wire id="temp[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the seventh partial product.</wire>
        <assign temp[6] = {{2{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg[6:0]} & {{2{1'b0}}, mul_b_reg[6:0]};>
        <wire id="temp[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Wire to hold the eighth partial product.</wire>
        <assign temp[7] = {{1{1'b0}}, mul_a_reg} & {{1{1'b0}}, mul_b_reg};>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="sum[0]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the first partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[1]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the second partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[2]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the third partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[3]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fourth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[4]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the fifth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[5]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the sixth partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[6]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the seventh partial sum.</reg>
        <reg id="sum[7]" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the eighth partial sum.</reg>
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                sum[0] <= 0;
                sum[1] <= 0;
                sum[2] <= 0;
                sum[3] <= 0;
                sum[4] <= 0;
                sum[5] <= 0;
                sum[6] <= 0;
                sum[7] <= 0;
            end else begin
                sum[0] <= temp[0];
                sum[1] <= sum[0] + temp[1];
                sum[2] <= sum[1] + temp[2];
                sum[3] <= sum[2] + temp[3];
                sum[4] <= sum[3] + temp[4];
                sum[5] <= sum[4] + temp[5];
                sum[6] <= sum[5] + temp[6];
                sum[7] <= sum[6] + temp[7];
            end
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="mul_out_reg" width_description="[15:0]">Register to hold the final product.</reg>
        <always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)>
            if (~rst_n) begin
                mul_out_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_out_reg <= sum[7];
            end
        </always>
    </logic_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign mul_out = mul_en_out ? mul_out_reg : 0;>
    </logic_description>
</module>
